article_title: Atacicept Cuts Proteinuria 46% in Phase 3 ORIGIN Trial for IgA Nephropathy, Setting Up a July FDA Decision

content_preview.title: Atacicept Cuts Proteinuria 46% in Phase 3 ORIGIN Trial for IgA Nephropathy, Setting Up a July FDA Decision
content_preview.summary: Vera Therapeutics' dual BAFF/APRIL inhibitor reduced proteinuria 45.7% at week 36 in the NEJM-published ORIGIN 3 trial, with an FDA accelerated-approval decision due July 7.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Atacicept, an experimental antibody that blocks two B-cell survival signals at once, significantly reduced proteinuria compared with placebo at week 36 in adults with IgA nephropathy, according to the Phase 3 ORIGIN 3 trial published in the *New England Journal of Medicine*. The interim analysis, summarized by [Cleveland Clinic Journal of Medicine](https://www.ccjm.org/page/asn-2025/atacicept-significantly), reported a 45.7% reduction in the 24-hour urinary protein-to-creatinine rat...

recommendations: Before merging, the human reviewer should write a corrections file at src/content/corrections/<YYYY-MM>/<article-slug>.json documenting the warning(s).
Consider adding trusted domains to config/source_allowlist.txt

editor_notes.content_quality: Clear, well-structured clinical news report (825 words, News category). Sections Overview / What We Know / Regulatory Path / What We Don't Know cleanly separate the verified interim efficacy data from the regulatory path and the explicit surrogate-endpoint caveat. Technical depth is appropriate for a clinical-trial story and every numeric specific is attributed inline to a named outlet.
editor_notes.source_verification: All 8 source snapshots read from disk by gunzip-ing each source-*.html.gz under sources/2026-06/atacicept-cuts-proteinuria-46-in-phase-3-origin-trial-for-iga-nephropathy-setting-up-a-july-fda-decision/. RULE 9 / RULE 5i CLINICAL VERIFICATION (every statistic traced verbatim to a FETCHABLE, non-403 snapshot): source-1 ccjm.org (HTTP 200) confirms verbatim 45.7% vs 6.8% proteinuria reduction, between-group difference 41.8 pp, P < .001, Gd-IgA1 -68.3% vs -2.9% with reductions by week 4, hematuria resolution 81.0% vs 20.7%, adverse events 59.3% vs 50.0%, 203 patients, atacicept 150 mg SC weekly, ASN Kidney Week 2025 in Houston. source-2 scholars.mssm.edu (HTTP 200) confirms verbatim the geometric mean between-group difference 41.8 pp (95% CI 28.9 to 52.3; P<0.001) AND the full NEJM citation: NEJM Vol. 394, No. 7, pp. 647-657, Published 12 Feb 2026, DOI 10.1056/NEJMoa2510198. source-0 nephjc.com (HTTP 200) confirms the 106-atacicept / 97-placebo split, that serious TEAEs were less frequent with atacicept (0.5% vs 5.1%), and the eGFR-data limitation framing. source-3 stocktitan.net (HTTP 200) confirms the BAFF + APRIL mechanism, the Marshall Fordyce quote verbatim ('We are excited for the potential to deliver the first approved therapy targeting both BAFF and APRIL in adults with IgAN'), and the eGFR-results-Q3-2026 / sBLA-Q4-2026 timing. source-4 pharmacally.com (HTTP 200) confirms the July 7, 2026 PDUFA target action date, Priority Review, Accelerated Approval pathway, BLA acceptance, and 'first B-cell modulator in IgAN to target both BAFF and APRIL'. source-5 ncbi.nlm.nih.gov StatPearls (HTTP 200) confirms ~10% US biopsies, up to 40% East Asia, 20-30% Europe, and 20-50% ESRD within 20 years. source-6 pharmacytimes.com (HTTP 200) confirms sibeprenlimab's November 2025 FDA accelerated approval, Voyxact/Otsuka branding, and first-in-class APRIL inhibitor. source-7 doi.org NEJM (Archive.org fallback, thin wrapper with no article specifics) is cited ONLY for the fact-of-publication, not for any unverifiable statistic — correct usage. NOTE: the task brief flagged NEJM/Vera-IR/HCPLive/Renal&Urology-News as 403; none of those are the cited sources here. The four outlets that quote the paper verbatim (NephJC, CCJM, Mount Sinai, plus StockTitan/Pharmacally/StatPearls/PharmacyTimes) all returned HTTP 200 and were read in full.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: No fabricated or misattributed specifics. Every number, name, date, and code traces verbatim to the snapshot it is attributed to. Headline '46%' vs body/summary '45.7%' is an acceptable, consistent rounding — the summary and body both carry the precise 45.7% figure and only the headline rounds. The single direct quote (Fordyce) is verbatim in StockTitan.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional. No sensationalism, no AI self-reference. The 'What We Don't Know' section appropriately foregrounds that proteinuria is a surrogate marker and that the lack of eGFR slope data is the reason for the accelerated (not full) pathway — well-calibrated for a regulatory-stage drug story.
editor_notes.originality: No prior coverage of atacicept, ORIGIN 3, or Vera Therapeutics in the archive. Adjacent Biotech/Drug Development coverage exists (obexelimab IgG4-RD Phase 3 NEJM, sibeprenlimab which the article correctly cites as the already-approved comparator, Agios mitapivat accelerated approval) but this is a distinct story with a distinct drug, trial, and FDA timeline.
editor_notes.human_requested_note: Not a human-requested article (human_requested: false). Standard scrutiny applied, with heightened Rule 9 clinical-specifics verification per the article's clinical-trial subject matter.
concerns: Five of the eight cited domains are not on config/source_allowlist.txt (nephjc.com, ccjm.org, scholars.mssm.edu, pharmacytimes.com, pharmacally.com). Four are clearly reputable clinical/academic outlets (NephJC nephrology journal club, Cleveland Clinic Journal of Medicine, Icahn School of Medicine at Mount Sinai institutional repository, Pharmacy Times trade publication). pharmacally.com is a smaller pharma-news aggregator and the weakest outlet in the set; however, the regulatory facts it sources (July 7 PDUFA, Priority Review, Accelerated Approval) are independently consistent with the StockTitan disclosure and were verified verbatim in its snapshot.
recommendations: Consider adding nephjc.com, ccjm.org, scholars.mssm.edu, and pharmacytimes.com to config/source_allowlist.txt — all are reputable clinical/academic sources likely to recur in Biotech & Medicine coverage.

For future clinical stories, prefer the primary peer-reviewed outlet or a top-tier trade outlet over smaller aggregators (e.g., pharmacally.com) for regulatory-milestone facts, even when the specific is independently confirmable.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: High-quality, accurate clinical-trial report. Every clinical statistic and the single quote trace verbatim to a fetchable (HTTP 200) snapshot; the NEJM DOI is correctly used only for fact-of-publication. The sole issue is the source-allowlist warning (chiefly the pharmacally.com aggregator), which the script flags as a warning and which is honestly summarizable in a corrections/provenance note. Verdict: APPROVE_WITH_CORRECTIONS.
